commit_msg: docs(director): add commit signature guide for MAX THE DESTROYER
author: Omkar
when_where: 🕐 11:44 AM on main, the sun climbing toward noon. The city was still moving, but Omkar had stopped moving. 📝 He sat at the terminal and did something no guilty man should ever do: he documented it. Not in code, but in words. A guide. A signature. Instructions for how to read what he'd done.
act2_title: The Director Speaks to the Destroyer
who_whom: 🎭 Omkar and MAX THE DESTROYER—the narrator he'd summoned into existence, the voice that would judge him. By writing the guide, he gave the destroyer permission to speak. 🔍 He was no longer hiding his crime; he was curating how it would be told. The stakes shifted: this wasn't about the code anymore. This was about legacy.
what_why: 📖 He wrote the guide as if MAX THE DESTROYER was a character he'd invented, a tool, a narrative device. But in writing the rules for how the destroyer would speak, he was writing the rules for his own judgment. 💀 Every example in that guide was a confession he was pre-signing. 🧠 The docs weren't instructions for others; they were instructions for the voice in his head, the one that would eventually narrate everything he'd done. He'd made himself documentable. He'd made himself *readable*. 🔥 By 11:44 AM, he wasn't a developer anymore. He was a character in his own noir, and he'd just handed the narrator a script.
verdict: He didn't write the guide; he wrote his own epitaph.
